creator: se@FreeBSD.org
text:
On May 29, admin@itacom.com.py wrote:
> Hello, I am a user of Freebsd, I use the operative system for
> to administer a node of Internet in Paraguay , we are ISP (internet service
> provider)
> I have 02 server with network adapter compatible NE2000 and work
> with TCP-IP and Ethernet to 10 Mbits.
> My problem is with a computer COMPAQ because does not recognize
> the network adaptere that has and it does not has slots ISA, alone EISA and PCI...

You should be able to use any ISA or PCI card in that 
system. EISA slots support ISA cards just fine.

But your Compaq may come with on-board Lance Ethernet 
(possibly in form of the AMD Ethernet SCSI Combo chip),
and you will be able to use that under FreeBSD, but will
have to configure the "lnc0" device driver for the 
non-standard port address used.

(I can check this out, if you send me a VERBOSE (!) boot
log (type "-v" at the "Boot: " prompt) from that Compaq.)

If you don't have that Lance compatible chip on your
system's motherboard, then depending on the network load
a simple PCI NE2000 clone (< US$25) will be sufficient.

I leave it to others to comment on bus-master PCI cards,
which are more appropriate for a server-class machine.

Regards, STefan
